name: Government Printing Office
description_short: Government Printing Office Federal Credit Union is a Washington, DC-based credit union offering comprehensive financial services including savings accounts, mortgages, auto loans, and investment planning to its membership.
description_long: Government Printing Office Federal Credit Union (GPO FCU) is a federally chartered credit union headquartered in Washington, DC, serving the Washington community and broader membership base. As a credit union, it operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA). The organization has been providing financial services tailored to personal and business needs within the DC area. GPO FCU offers a comprehensive suite of banking and lending products including high-yield checking and savings accounts, flexible auto loans, and mortgage options. Their savings products range from regular share savings accounts (4,113 active accounts) to money market accounts ($2.7M+ in assets across 53 accounts), share certificates/CDs (138 active accounts), and retirement accounts including IRAs and KEOGH plans (73 active accounts). They provide three mortgage options: fixed-rate mortgages under 15 years, fixed-rate mortgages over 15 years, and adjustable-rate mortgages. Digital banking services include mobile banking, online banking, bill pay, and a membership application portal. As a credit union, GPO FCU distinguishes itself through member-owned governance and competitive rates typically lower than traditional banks. The organization emphasizes personalized service, dedicated financial planning, wealth management, exclusive member benefits and discounts, and robust digital capabilities. Their focus on the Washington, DC community with branch locator services and local expertise provides personalized attention compared to large national banks. While GPO FCU provides legitimate credit union services with NCUA oversight and documented membership (4,000+ savings accounts across multiple product lines), the website offers limited specific rate information, APR details, or fee schedules. Prospective members should request detailed product specifications before opening accounts. The credit union appears well-established but detailed financial stability metrics and customer service reviews were not presented on the reviewed website content.
pros: NCUA-insured federally chartered credit union with documented active membership across multiple account types
Competitive mortgage options including fixed-rate (15+ years and under 15 years) and adjustable-rate mortgages
Multiple savings vehicles documented by NCUA: regular savings (4,113 accounts), money market accounts ($2.7M+ assets), CDs (138 accounts), and retirement accounts
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile app, online banking, and bill pay services
Member-owned credit union structure typically offers lower fees and better rates than traditional banks
Dedicated financial planning and wealth management services beyond basic banking
Auto loan and personal loan products with flexible terms
cons: Website lacks specific APR rates, interest rate tiers, and fee schedules for any products
Limited information on loan eligibility requirements, credit score minimums, or membership restrictions
No specific timeline or cost details provided for mortgage processing or auto loan approval
Website does not display current promotional offers, welcome bonuses, or member incentives
Membership application requirements and field of membership eligibility criteria not clearly detailed on reviewed content
best_for: Washington, DC-area residents and workers seeking comprehensive credit union banking with local branch access
Home buyers looking for mortgage products with personalized service and competitive rates
Members prioritizing member-owned financial institution structures and potential lower fees
Savers building long-term wealth through retirement accounts, CDs, and money market products
diagnosis: GPO FCU is best for DC-area residents and those seeking member-owned credit union banking with diverse savings and lending products. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal rate, fee, or eligibility information—prospective members should contact branches directly for specific product terms and current offerings before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
